mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% on 2019-11-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations over a 60-day forward horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth rate exceeds 0.15% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,252 thousand on 2019-11-01 reflects sustained employment gains
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns drops below 0.10 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,314 thousand on 2019-10-01 shows housing resilience
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between RSAFS and HOUST declines is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.67 on 2019-10-01 reflects manufacturing activity
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO spikes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, the Federal Reserve is expected to cut rates (FEDFUNDS), signaling a policy pivot toward accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and FEDFUNDS subsequently declines by at least 0.25pp within 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% on 2019-11-01 remains below trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary policy pivot from tightening to easing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of UNRATE and FEDFUNDS changes is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2.0% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors reprice growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,122.06 billion on 2019-07-01 reflects economic expansion
regime_hint: above-trend economic growth or late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns drops below 0.12 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward in anticipation of weakening consumer demand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% on 2019-11-01 is near cyclical lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE increases and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates (FEDFUNDS), which increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice valuations downward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS remains above 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 257.936 on 2019-11-01 reflects moderate inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle or inflation-driven policy constraint
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of CPI acceleration and elevated FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.
